notes: All three Julia-style initial conditions were run for the pure Python baseline.
Only IC 0 currently has a faithful paired C++ memory run because the ABI exposes the built-in Lorenz63 scenario initial state but not custom single-level initial-condition overrides.
Spectral diagnosis is shown explicitly using the current built-in Lorenz63 memory kernel metadata mirrored from the core source.

baseline_lyapunov.note: Approximate largest Lyapunov estimate from paired deterministic trajectories.

memory_lyapunov.note: Current ABI does not expose step-level/full-trajectory Lorenz63 state needed for faithful memory-side Lyapunov estimation.

per_ic.note: Memory run available because the built-in C++ Lorenz63 scenario uses the same default initial state [1,1,1].

spectral_diagnosis.note: Current ABI does not expose the single-level Lorenz63 kernel directly; these values mirror the built-in scenario defaults.

baseline_lyapunov.note: Approximate largest Lyapunov estimate from paired deterministic trajectories.

memory_lyapunov.note: Current single-level Lorenz63 ABI path does not permit overriding the C++ scenario initial condition.

per_ic.note: Baseline completed; memory-side run withheld to avoid misrepresenting IC coverage until the ABI exposes custom single-level initial states.
